step_by_step_reasoning: The whiteboard displays a supply chain material traceability architecture. Four user roles (Project Manager, Supplier, Distributor, and Utility) interact with the system. An SAP ERP system initiates purchase orders, integrating with Amazon Managed Blockchain via API. AWS IoT Core captures physical telemetry (location and handoffs) and pushes it to the blockchain ledger (running Hyperledger Fabric) to maintain an immutable single source of truth, while sending notifications to users when thresholds are breached.

graph.name: PwC Blockchain-Powered Material Traceability
graph.notes: Integrates SAP enterprise workflows with Amazon Managed Blockchain and AWS IoT Core to provide end-to-end supply chain transparency.

nodes.name: SAP ERP
nodes.notes: Triggers the workflow via API call when a purchase order is created.

nodes.name: Amazon Managed Blockchain
nodes.notes: Runs Hyperledger Fabric 1.2 to store immutable ledger state and run chaincode.

nodes.name: AWS IoT Core
nodes.notes: Ingests physical asset telemetry and triggers blockchain updates based on thresholds.

edges.notes: PM creates Purchase Order in SAP.
API call publishes PO to blockchain.
Publishes sensor telemetry and asset handoffs to the ledger.
Sends real-time alerts or mobile notifications for threshold breaches.
